backlog.title: Initialise project with Vite, React, TypeScript, and CRXJS
backlog.description: Scaffold the extension project using Vite with @crxjs/vite-plugin. Configure TypeScript, React, and the Manifest V3 entry points (background service worker, content script, popup). Install core dependencies: react, react-dom, d3, webextension-polyfill.
acceptance: npm create vite scaffolds a working TypeScript + React project
@crxjs/vite-plugin is installed and configured in vite.config.ts
manifest.json is valid Manifest V3 with permissions: storage, alarms, activeTab
host_permissions include ip2location.io, electricitymap.org, and thegreenwebfoundation.org
Background service worker, content script, and popup entry points are wired up
npm run dev starts the extension in development mode with hot reload
Extension loads successfully as an unpacked extension in Chrome

backlog.title: Configure environment variables and API key management
backlog.description: Set up .env file for API keys (ip2location.io, Electricity Maps). Create .env.example with placeholder values. Add .env to .gitignore. Wire VITE_* env vars through to the background worker.
acceptance: .env.example exists with VITE_IP2LOCATION_API_KEY and VITE_ELECTRICITY_MAPS_API_KEY placeholders
.env is in .gitignore
Environment variables are accessible in the background service worker via import.meta.env
Build fails clearly if required API keys are missing

backlog.title: Configure testing infrastructure (Vitest + Playwright)
backlog.description: Install and configure Vitest for unit tests and Playwright for cross-browser E2E tests. Set up test directory structure, mock chrome.* APIs via webextensions-jsdom, and add npm scripts for test/test:e2e.
acceptance: Vitest is configured with TypeScript support
webextensions-jsdom is installed for mocking chrome.* APIs in unit tests
Playwright is configured for Chrome and Firefox
npm run test runs unit tests
npm run test:e2e runs E2E tests
A sample smoke test passes in both frameworks

backlog.title: Set up project folder structure and shared modules
backlog.description: Create the directory layout: src/background/, src/content/, src/popup/components/, src/popup/hooks/, src/shared/, tests/unit/, tests/e2e/, icons/. Scaffold shared/types.ts with core TypeScript interfaces and shared/constants.ts with grade thresholds, badge colours, and conversion factors.
acceptance: All directories from the project structure exist
shared/types.ts exports interfaces: PageResult, DailyTotal, GeoCache, GreenCache, ResourceEntry
shared/constants.ts exports grade thresholds, badge colour map, and real-world comparison factors
tsconfig.json path aliases are configured for @background/, @content/, @popup/, @shared/

backlog.title: Collect page resource data via PerformanceResourceTiming API
backlog.description: Content script reads window.performance.getEntriesByType('resource') on page load complete, extracts transferSize and decodedBodySize per resource, and categorises by type (image, script, font, CSS, other).
acceptance: Content script fires after page load complete
All resource entries are captured with transferSize in bytes
Resources are categorised into: images, scripts, fonts, CSS, other
Data is sent to background worker via chrome.runtime.sendMessage

backlog.title: IP geolocation lookup for server hosting country
backlog.description: Background worker extracts domain from page URL and resolves hosting country via ip2location.io API. Results are cached in chrome.storage.local for 24 hours.
acceptance: Domain extracted from document.location.hostname
ip2location.io API called with hostname (not IP)
Response cached under geo_cache:{domain} with 24-hour expiry
Cached result is used on subsequent lookups within TTL

backlog.title: Carbon intensity lookup via Electricity Maps API
backlog.description: Background worker maps the hosting country to real-time grid carbon intensity (gCO2/kWh) using the Electricity Maps API. Results are cached hourly.
acceptance: Electricity Maps API called with country code from geolocation result
Returns carbon intensity in gCO2/kWh
Response cached for 1 hour
Fallback to global average (442 gCO2/kWh) if API is unavailable

backlog.title: Green hosting check via Green Web Foundation API
backlog.description: Background worker checks whether the page's hosting provider is on the Green Web Foundation green hosting registry. Returns a boolean. Cached per domain.
acceptance: Green Web Foundation API called with domain
Boolean is_green stored under green_cache:{domain}
Cache has configurable TTL (default 7 days)
Green host status is factored into carbon calculation

backlog.title: Carbon calculation engine
backlog.description: Implement the websitecarbon.com open methodology: co2_grams = (data_gb * 0.81 * visitor_factor * carbon_intensity) + (data_gb * 0.52 * 0.25 * 442). Returning visitor adjustment is 0.75x for previously visited pages. Green hosting zeroes out data centre energy component.
acceptance: Formula matches websitecarbon.com methodology
Returning visitor factor (0.75x) applied for repeat visits
Green hosting multiplier zeroes data centre energy portion
Output includes: co2_grams, energy_kwh, transfer_kb, grade

backlog.title: Letter grade assignment (A+ through F)
backlog.description: Assign a letter grade based on CO2 per page load, using thresholds aligned with websitecarbon.com methodology for credibility. Generate percentile comparison against all previously analysed pages.
acceptance: Grades range from A+ (cleanest) to F (dirtiest)
Thresholds are consistent with websitecarbon.com grading
Percentile comparison text generated: 'heavier than X% of N pages analysed'
Grade stored alongside page result in chrome.storage.local

backlog.title: Store page results in chrome.storage.local
backlog.description: Persist per-page analysis results using the schema: page:{domain}:{date} -> {grade, co2_grams, transfer_kb, visits, green_host}. Update running totals under totals:{YYYY-MM-DD}.
acceptance: Page results stored under page:{domain}:{date} key
Daily totals updated under totals:{YYYY-MM-DD} key
Visit count incremented on repeat visits to same domain/date
Storage remains within chrome.storage.local 10MB limit

backlog.title: Extension badge update with page grade
backlog.description: Update the extension icon badge text and colour after each page analysis. Use chrome.action.setBadgeText and setBadgeBackgroundColor. Colour scheme: #27AE60 (A+/A), #F39C12 (B/C), #E74C3C (D/F).
acceptance: Badge text shows current page grade (A+, A, B, C, D, F)
Badge background colour is green (#27AE60) for A+/A
Badge background colour is amber (#F39C12) for B/C
Badge background colour is red (#E74C3C) for D/F
Badge updates automatically on each page load

backlog.title: Current page score and grade display
backlog.description: Popup shows the current page's carbon score, letter grade, transfer size (KB/MB), estimated energy (kWh), CO2 equivalent (grams), green hosting status, and percentile comparison.
acceptance: Displays letter grade with colour-coded badge
Shows total transfer size in KB or MB
Shows estimated CO2 in grams
Shows estimated energy in kWh
Shows green hosting status (green/conventional)
Shows percentile comparison text

backlog.title: Resource breakdown chart
backlog.description: Show a breakdown of the current page's resources by type (images, scripts, fonts, CSS, other) with transfer sizes, so users can see what is heaviest.
acceptance: Breakdown shows each resource category with size in KB
Categories: images, scripts, fonts, CSS, other
Sorted by size descending (heaviest first)
Visual bar or chart representation of proportions

backlog.title: Historical totals view (daily/weekly/monthly)
backlog.description: Dashboard section in popup showing cumulative browsing footprint aggregated by day, week, and month in grams CO2.
acceptance: Toggle between daily, weekly, and monthly views
Displays total CO2 in grams for selected period
Displays total page count for selected period
Data sourced from totals:{YYYY-MM-DD} storage entries

backlog.title: Top 10 heaviest sites chart (D3.js)
backlog.description: Horizontal bar chart rendered with D3.js showing the user's top 10 heaviest sites ranked by average carbon per visit.
acceptance: D3.js horizontal bar chart renders in popup
Shows top 10 domains ranked by average CO2 per visit
Each bar labelled with domain name and average grams CO2
Chart is responsive within popup dimensions

backlog.title: Annual estimate with real-world comparisons
backlog.description: Extrapolate current browsing data to an estimated annual footprint with relatable comparisons: km driven, flights taken, meals of beef.
acceptance: Annual CO2 estimate extrapolated from available data
Comparison to km driven in an average car
Comparison to short-haul flights
Comparison to meals of beef
Comparisons use standard conversion factors

backlog.title: Export shareable footprint summary
backlog.description: Export button generates a 'My browsing footprint report' as a shareable summary (image or text) showing key stats from the user's browsing history.
acceptance: Export button visible in dashboard view
Generates a summary with total CO2, page count, top sites, grade distribution
Output is shareable (clipboard text or downloadable image)
Includes CarbonStamp branding

backlog.title: Per-resource breakdown with optimisation suggestions
backlog.description: Developer mode shows granular per-resource data and flags optimisation opportunities: uncompressed images, render-blocking scripts, no HTTP/2, missing CDN.
acceptance: Toggle to enable developer mode in popup
Lists individual resources with size, type, and protocol
Flags uncompressed images (no content-encoding)
Flags render-blocking scripts
Flags resources not served over HTTP/2
Flags resources not served via CDN
Shows estimated carbon saving if optimisations applied

backlog.title: 90-day data cleanup via chrome.alarms
backlog.description: Implement a daily cleanup job using chrome.alarms API to prune storage entries older than 90 days, keeping chrome.storage.local within the 10MB limit.
acceptance: chrome.alarms fires daily cleanup
Entries with dates older than 90 days are removed
Both page:{domain}:{date} and totals:{YYYY-MM-DD} entries are pruned
Expired geo_cache and green_cache entries are also cleaned

backlog.title: Firefox compatibility
backlog.description: Ensure the extension works on Firefox using browser.* namespace or webextension-polyfill. Test and adapt manifest for Firefox Add-ons submission.
acceptance: Extension loads and runs correctly in Firefox
All chrome.* API calls work via browser.* or polyfill
Manifest v2/v3 compatibility handled
Submitted to Firefox Add-ons store

backlog.title: Publish to Chrome Web Store
backlog.description: Package extension and publish to Chrome Web Store with store listing that includes the Green Atlas narrative.
acceptance: Extension packaged as .zip for Chrome Web Store
Store listing includes description, screenshots, and Green Atlas narrative
Privacy policy reflects local-only data storage
Published and publicly available
